name: Van Berg Municipal Golf Course
about: Quail Run Golf Course is a public golf facility established in 1991, recognized as one of Nebraska's top-ranked public courses. The course features a challenging layout of 5 tee boxes ranging from 5,200 to 7,200 yards, designed to test the skills of all players. It provides a unique playing experience carved through towering cottonwoods along the Loup River and the historic Old Mormon Trail. The venue offers a variety of amenities including a driving range, golf academy, and facilities for events and tournaments, making it suitable for both individual play and group outings.
why_golfers_like_it: Challenging course with 5 tee boxes for all skill levels
Scenic location along the Loup River and Old Mormon Trail
Variety of leagues and competitive events available
Comprehensive golf academy and coaching options
Accessible tee time policy (7 days in advance)

food_and_drink.notes: On-site dining and cook-outs for events mentioned
Alcohol service implied for events and clubhouse

simulator_hardware.notes: Available in the Van Berg Family Golf Center & TRACKman Lounge

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, players are expected to bring their own golf clubs, though club rentals may be available through the pro shop.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: Outside food and drink policies are not explicitly stated, but the venue offers on-site dining and cook-outs for events.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The time to play 18 holes is not specified, but the course offers a challenging 7,200-yard layout from the tips.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue offers youth programs, including summer golf day camps and a PGA Junior League for ages 16 and under.
